title: PTC Inc. (PTC) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of PTC Inc. (PTC) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 10% growth rate is projected based on the continued expansion of high-margin subscription revenues and increasing adoption of SaaS solutions, driving margin expansion.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects a moderate risk profile. The stability of recurring enterprise software revenues is balanced against the cyclicality of its end markets in manufacturing.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3% terminal growth rate assumes PTC will grow slightly faster than long-term GDP, reflecting the enduring nature of its software in the industrial sector.

thesisParagraphs: PTC has successfully transformed its business model from selling perpetual licenses to a recurring subscription and SaaS model. This shift not only provides greater revenue visibility but also enhances the lifetime value of its customer base.

The company is well-positioned to benefit from secular tailwinds in industrial digitalization. As manufacturers increasingly adopt digital twins, IoT, and augmented reality to optimize operations, PTC's comprehensive suite of solutions places it at the center of this transformation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What drives PTC's free cash flow growth?
faq.answer: Growth is primarily driven by the transition of its customer base to higher-value SaaS and subscription models, which offer better long-term unit economics and higher gross margins.

faq.question: How does PTC's moat protect its valuation?
faq.answer: High switching costs protect its valuation. Once deeply embedded in a manufacturer's core processes, replacing CAD or PLM software is incredibly disruptive, ensuring high customer retention rates.

faq.question: What are the primary risks to this valuation?
faq.answer: A severe economic downturn that curtails capital expenditure and IT budgets in the manufacturing sector is the primary risk, potentially slowing new software deployments.
